column1: # 亞特蘭提斯之謎 繁體中文譯表(source,UTF-8)
# 格式:英文原文 <TAB> 繁體中文。# 開頭為註解,空行略過。
# 鍵 = 遊戲 convertMessageToString 後的「單行」顯示字串(多行/含控制碼的對白暫不放這)。
# 由 tools/build_translation.py 編成 Big5 的 atlantis_zh.tab(放進 game/)。
# 譯名依 CONTEXT.md:瓊斯 / 蘇菲亞 / 亞特蘭提斯 / 納粹。
It's my favorite piece of equipment.
# ── 開場 / 紐約場景物件名(角型雕像線索鏈)──────────
I have a hand full of beads.
I can't do this by myself.
I can't reach it from here.
I can't lift the whole thing at once.
I don't want to be shot.
I think I'll need something heavier.
column2: 得找個更有份量的傢伙。

column1: I'd better find that stone key first.
column2: 我最好先找到那把石鑰匙。

column1: I'd better pour out the water first.
He won't miss a few bones.
column2: 少他幾根骨頭他也不會在意。

column1: # ── 對白 / 檢視回應(多句用 \n 斷行;印第口吻 + 黑色幽默)──────
It's my old ice box. I haven't used it in months.
column2: 我那台老冰箱。\n好幾個月沒碰過了。

column1: It's a window. I can't see through it.
column2: 一扇窗。\n我又沒有透視眼。

column1: It's locked. I'll need some sort of key to unlock it.
column2: 鎖著。\n得弄把鑰匙才打得開。

column1: It looks like the torpedo controls. They're very complicated.
column2: 看來是魚雷控制台。\n複雜得要命。

column1: I can't. There's something on the pedestal already.
column2: 不行,\n基座上已經擺了東西。

column1: It's the Lost Dialogue of Plato.
column2: 這是柏拉圖失落的對話錄。

column1: Why would I want to do that?
# ── 開場旁白(印第內心獨白,自動播放;\n 斷行)──────
Well, here I am in New York wondering how Sophia Hapgood got mixed up with Nazi spies.
column2: 好啦,我人是到紐約了。\n真搞不懂蘇菲亞·哈普古德\n怎麼會跟納粹間諜攪和到一塊去。

column1: # ── 開場對白(runtime 攔截;印第口吻 + 黑色幽默)──────
Alright, Jones How are you going to find that STATUE in all this JUNK?
column2: 好啦,瓊斯,\n你是打算怎麼在這一堆破爛裡\n翻出那尊雕像?

column1: You don't look at all well, Dr. Jones.
column2: 你的氣色可不太好啊,瓊斯博士。

column1: # ── 高頻通用回應 / 對白(runtime-match key,印第口吻)──────
I can't get there from here.
It's a chain with one end looped through the statue arm.
column2: 一條鐵鍊,一端套在雕像手臂上。

column1: I don't need any old bones.
It doesn't seem to fit properly.
But I know he has an aquiline nose.
column2: 不過我知道他是鷹鉤鼻。

column1: But he always wears a flower in his lapel.
column2: 不過他西裝領上總別著一朵花。

column1: I need to move closer first.

column2: 我還是別打擾他們好了。

column1: Look for a man in a brown suit.
column2: 找一個穿棕色西裝的男人。

column1: Looks like a cave to me.
I don't think that will help me here.
column2: 我看這在這兒派不上用場。

column1: I don't think that will work.
# ── 劇情 / 謎題對白(freq≥3,印第口吻 + 黑色幽默)──────
Can you understand what this mural means?
column2: 你看得懂這幅壁畫的意思嗎?

column1: Do you know where to find Omar Al-Jabbar?
column2: 你知道哪裡找得到歐瑪·賈巴爾嗎?

column1: Do you think the Nazis have been here?
column2: 你覺得納粹來過這兒嗎?

column1: Here we are in the ruins of Knossos.
column2: 我們來到了克諾索斯的遺跡。

column1: I better not touch it I don't know what might happen.
column2: 我最好別碰,天曉得會出什麼事。

column1: I can't give away pots that aren't mine.
column2: 不是我的罐子,我可不能隨便送人。

column1: I don't want to talk to an empty pit.
column2: 我才不要對著一個空坑說話。

column1: I hope this is the way out.
I'd better get down from here first.
I'd rather not stick my hand under a stream of molten lava.
column2: 我可不想把手伸進岩漿裡。

column1: I'll have to get closer than this.
I'm not sure, but I suspect we've already found what we need.
column2: 我不確定,但我猜我們要的東西已經到手了。

column1: I'm not sure. Maybe we should try using the stones again.
column2: 我不確定,也許該再試試那些石盤。

column1: In the last days of Atlantis.
column2: 在亞特蘭提斯的最後歲月。

column1: It must have been part of some ancient machine.
column2: 這一定是某台古代機器的零件。

column1: It won't go down any further.
It won't go up any further.
It's a chain through a loop of stone.
It's a chain with one end looped through the door loop.
column2: 一條鐵鍊,一端套在門環上。

column1: It's a diagram showing how to find the Moonstone.
# ── 劇情 / 謎題對白 2(船名、檢視、印第口吻)──────
It won't go any further in that direction.
It won't go any further that way.
It's an arm of the statue with one end of the chain looped through it.
column2: 雕像的手臂,鐵鍊一端套在上面。
看著像隻叢林裡的嚙齒動物。

column1: I believe BARNETT COLLEGE owns the collection!
column2: 我相信這批收藏歸巴奈特大學所有!

column1: It's a path leading deeper into the jungle.
column2: 一條通往叢林深處的路。

column1: It's too dark to see anything down there.
column2: 下面太暗,什麼都看不見。

column1: I can't pull an empty slot.
I can't push an empty slot.
I can't see what I'm doing.
I don't think that will do any good.
I'd rather not stick my hand into a stream of hot lava.
column2: 我可不想把手伸進滾燙的岩漿裡。

column1: I'm not yours to give, thank you very much.
column2: 我可不是你能送的,多謝囉。

column1: It can't move any more in that direction.
It feels like a spark plug.
It's an arm of the statue.
The door seems to be locked.
The remains of the chain are still hooked to the arm.
column2: 殘餘的鐵鍊還掛在手臂上。
這兒沒什麼要緊的東西。

column1: # ── 對話(角色交談;印第口吻)──────
Ah, Monsieur Trottier I'm so pleased to meet such an expert antiquarian at long last!
column2: 啊,特羅蒂耶先生,總算見到您這位古董行家了,真是三生有幸!

column1: Are you sure you know what you're doing?
column2: 你確定你知道自己在幹嘛?

column1: At least there's some evidence this time.
Can you get it for me?
Can you tell me where to find Monsieur Trottier?
column2: 你能告訴我哪裡找得到特羅蒂耶先生嗎?

column1: Come on, Sophia, let's get out of here.
column2: 走吧,蘇菲亞,我們離開這兒。

column1: Do you know anything about Atlantis?
column2: 你知道亞特蘭提斯的事嗎?

column1: Do you know what's in it?
column2: 你知道裡面裝了什麼嗎?

column1: # ── 對白(劇情/謎題/Nur-Ab-Sal riddle;印第口吻)──────
According to Nur-Ab-Sal, they were nothing but the Atlanteans' country cousins.
column2: 照 Nur-Ab-Sal 的說法,他們不過是亞特蘭提斯人的鄉下親戚罷了。

column1: And take a chance you'll get hopelessly lost? Not on your life, buster.
column2: 然後冒著迷路的險?休想,老兄。

column1: I can't go this way. It's too far to jump, and there's no place to whip.
column2: 這條路走不了。跳太遠,又沒地方甩鞭子。

column1: I don't know, but we should be careful. Something tells me they're not far away.
column2: 我不知道,但我們得小心。我有預感他們就在附近。

column1: I'm not sure. Maybe we should try using the Moonstone with the Sunstone.
column2: 我不確定,也許該試試把月石和日石一起用。

column1: It's pointing toward that door in the back of the room.
column2: 它指著房間後頭那扇門。

column1: It's the door to some shop.
It's the former Nazi dig site.
Let's figure out what the mural means.
column2: 來搞懂這幅壁畫的意思吧。

column1: Looks like a path into the mountain range.
column2: 看著像條通往山脈的路。

column1: Maybe there's something useful hidden among these ruins.
column2: 說不定這片廢墟裡藏著有用的東西。

column1: No thanks. I don't want it.
Radio? Signals from the fillings in your teeth?
column2: 收音機?從你補牙的填料收到的訊號?

column1: So you took my lamp, eh?
column2: 這麼說,你拿了我的燈,嗯?

column1: Some palace the Minoans had, huh?
column2: 米諾斯人這宮殿還真不賴,嗯?
還是這麼貧嘴。你會學乖的。

column1: The chain is already on the loop.
The stairway is closed for repairs.
This isn't working. Let's move on.
To place their city atop Mt. Olympus.
column2: 為了把城市建在奧林帕斯山頂。

column1: To stop the sea level rising.
Well, I've got to get to the other side somehow.
column2: 唉,我總得想辦法到對面去。

column1: When darkness concealed the tall horns.
column2: 當黑暗掩去高聳的牛角。

column1: When night ruled the Great City.
When the celestial spheres were well aligned.
Where do you think you're going?
Why are we here? Crete can't possibly be Atlantis.
column2: 我們來這兒幹嘛?克里特島不可能是亞特蘭提斯。

column1: # ── 配音試播段(印第看雜誌照片;對應 game/voice/indy_photo.voc)──────
This was taken a long while ago when I thought we might like each other.
column2: 這是很久以前拍的,那時我還以為,我們倆會看對眼。
